messages.content:
Who said that everything changes—“You cannot step into the same river twice.”
Group of answer choices

Democritus

Anaxagoras

Heraclitus

Parmenides
Heraclitus is the philosopher credited with saying that everything changes, and "You cannot step into the same river twice."

Who described the race that cannot be won by Achilles where he can never overtake the turtle?
Group of answer choices

Democritus

Zeno

Pythagoras

Hesiod
Zeno of Elea described the race in which Achilles can never overtake the turtle, known as one of Zeno's paradoxes.

Who thought the world process was illustrated in evaporation and condensation.
Group of answer choices

Democritus

Thales

Heraclitus

Socrates
Thales thought the world process was illustrated in evaporation and condensation.

Who of the following thought that only being is, that non-being (nothing) cannot be? 
Group of answer choices

Parmenides

Plato

Democritus

Empedocles
Parmenides is the philosopher who thought that only being is, and that non-being (nothing) cannot be.

Who thought that the basic world stuff is Air (pneuma)? [In today's world, we all drive cars with pneumatic tires which means they are filled with (pneuma) air.]
Group of answer choices

Anaximenes

Democritus

Zeno

Thales
Anaximenes thought that the basic world stuff is Air (pneuma).

Who thought that the nature of the world process as a whirling motion initiated by mind?
Group of answer choices

Anaxagoras

Zeno

Empedocles

Parmenides
Anaxagoras thought that the nature of the world process as a whirling motion initiated by mind.

Who thought that the “roots” of the world were four, that is,  Air, Earth, Fire and Water?
Group of answer choices

Anaxagoras

Empedocles

Democritus

Heraclitus
Empedocles thought that the “roots” of the world were four, that is, Air, Earth, Fire, and Water.

Who thought that the world stuff is the “Boundless?” (The Aperon)
Group of answer choices

Pythagoras

Anaxemines

Anaxamander

Thales
Anaximander thought that the world stuff is the "Boundless" (The Apeiron).

Who thought that the world process operates by repulsion (hate) and attraction (love?)
Group of answer choices

Empedocles

Heraclitus

Parmenides

Anaxagoras
Empedocles thought that the world process operates by repulsion (hate) and attraction (love).

Who thought the world of things was made up of infinite and various “seeds.”
Group of answer choices

Aristotle

Democritus

Anaxagoras

Thales
Anaxagoras thought the world of things was made up of infinite and various "seeds."

Who thought that the “odd” numbers present the image of order or goodness while even numbers give the image of chaos?
Group of answer choices

Xenophanes

Parmenides

Pythagoras

Plato
Pythagoras thought that the "odd" numbers present the image of order or goodness, while even numbers give the image of chaos.

Who thought that the real was only Atoms and the Void?
Group of answer choices

Democritus

Anaxagoras

Anaximander

Zeno
Democritus thought that the real was only Atoms and the Void.

Who thought the image of the world process is fire?
Group of answer choices

Democritus

Empedocles

Heraclitus

Thales
Heraclitus thought the image of the world process is fire.
